title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Gum Swelling
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great way to relieve gum swelling. You might be wondering how this works, so let's take a closer look. Gum swelling, or gingivitis, is often caused by infection or inflammation in the gums. This can be painful and uncomfortable, making it hard to eat or even speak. Herbal teas, made from certain plants, can help to reduce this swelling and ease the discomfort.  One example is Eucalyptus globulus, also known as the blue gum eucalyptus. This plant has natural antibacterial properties that can help to fight off the infection causing the swelling. Its essential oil can be inhaled to help ease congestion and reduce inflammation in the gums. Another herb that's often used is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. It's also known to have antibacterial properties, making it a great tool in the fight against gum infection.  Finally, there's Salvia officinalis, or sage. This herb has been used for centuries to treat a range of health issues, including gum problems. Its essential oil can be used to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums, making it a great natural remedy for gum swelling. By using these herbal essential oils, you can help to reduce gum swelling and ease the discomfort associated with it. This can be especially helpful if you have sensitive gums or are prone to gum infections. By using these natural remedies, you can take control of your oral health and reduce the risk of more serious problems down the line.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for gum swelling

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and limonene. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Eucalyptol, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation. The antiseptic properties of alpha-pinene and limonene help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling. By applying eucalyptus globulus essential oils topically, you can take advantage of these properties to help soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat, then add the Eucalyptus leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes. Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh.
Collect the liquid and transfer it to a glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dark place. Let it sit for 2-3 days to allow the oil to separate.
Carefully pour the oil from the top layer of the liquid into another glass bottle. Discard the remaining liquid and store the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains bioactive constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of gingerol and shogaol also help reduce redness and ease discomfort in the gums. Additionally, Zingiber officinale essential oils has antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and swelling. Regular use of Zingiber officinale essential oils may help reduce gum inflammation and promote healthy gums.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots and 2 cups of water.
Chop the ginger roots into small pieces and place them in a saucepan.
Add the 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, then let cool.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the ginger solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains compounds like borneol, camphor, and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and pain by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. The antiseptic properties of Salvia officinalis essential oils also help prevent infection and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, the oils' ability to relax muscles and reduce stress can contribute to a decrease in gum swelling by minimizing tension in the jaw and face. The combined effect of these properties makes Salvia officinalis essential oils a useful natural remedy for gum swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of water, a pot, and a distillation apparatus.
Wash the Salvia leaves with water and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves and water in the pot and heat it over low heat.
Connect the distillation apparatus to the pot and collect the steam that rises from the mixture.
Separate the essential oil from the water in the distillation apparatus and store it in a clean bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a bioactive compound called curcumin, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of inflammatory enzymes. The antioxidant properties of curcumin also help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, curcuma longa essential oils contain another compound called turmerone, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and heal gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, curcuma longa essential oils can help to alleviate gum swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Curcuma longa roots and wash them with water.
Dry the roots in the sun for 2 days or use a food dehydrator at 40°C (104°F) for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 cup of the powder with 4 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, then strain and store the essential oil in a glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains compounds like camphor, bornyl acetate, and limonene, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce gum swelling by fighting off bacteria and other pathogens that can cause inflammation. The essential oils' ability to reduce inflammation is also attributed to its antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils work together to promote a healthy environment for the gums to heal, reducing swelling and discomfort. By using Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils, you can potentially alleviate gum swelling and promote overall gum health.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers, and 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Wash the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers with clean water to remove dirt and impurities.
Add 1 cup of the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers to a glass jar, then pour 1 cup of carrier oil over them.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, and discard the solids. Your Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil is now ready to use for gum swelling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains active constituents like cineole, limonene, and beta-pinene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce gum swelling caused by infection or irritation. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gum inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils help to eliminate the underlying cause of gum swelling, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. Regular use of this essential oil may also help to reduce the severity of gum swelling and promote overall oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan to 180°F (82°C).
Add the Melaleuca leaves to the hot water and let it steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the leaves.
Let the mixture sit for 24 hours to allow the essential oil to separate and settle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils contains a compound called eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum swelling. Eugenol also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause gum inflammation. The essential oil also contains cineole, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums. These properties, combined with eugenol's ability to numb the area and reduce pain, make Syzygium aromaticum essential oils a useful treatment for gum swelling. Regular use of the essential oil can help keep gum inflammation under control and promote healthy gums.
remedies.image_alt: Syzygium aromaticum herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Get 2 cups of Syzygium aromaticum flowers (Clove buds) from a trusted supplier.
Use a mortar and pestle to gently crush 100g of the Syzygium aromaticum flowers.
Combine the crushed flowers with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 2 hours. Then, let the mixture cool and sit for 4 hours.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container. Discard the solids. Use 1 part essential oil to 3 parts carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to dilute the essential oil for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling. The rosmarinic acid in this oil also has antioxidant properties, which can help fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections. The essential oil's antimicrobial properties can also help prevent the spread of infection and promote a healthy environment for gum healing. Echinacea purpurea's ability to boost the immune system can also help gum tissue recover from infection. This oil's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties work together to help reduce swelling and promote gum health.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba oil or coconut oil), and a dark glass bottle with a lid.
Combine the Echinacea flowers and carrier oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle.
Store the bottle in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day. This allows the essential oil to mature and separate from the plant material.

After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil again and discard the plant material. Your Echinacea purpurea essential oil is now ready to use for gum swelling. Use 2-3 drops diluted with water before brushing teeth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two main active constituents that help reduce gum swelling. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can decrease swelling and redness in the mouth. Linalool also has antiseptic properties, helping to prevent infection and promoting a healthy environment for gum healing. Additionally, linalyl acetate has a calming effect, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum swelling. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help alleviate gum swelling and promote gum health.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for gum swelling
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers, a clean glass jar, and a cheesecloth.
Combine the flowers and 1 cup of vodka in the jar. Stir well and seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it every day. This will help extract the oil from the flowers.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of glycerin to the liquid and stir well. Your Lavandula angustifolia essential oil is now ready to use for gum swelling.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce gum swelling. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe swollen gums. They can also help calm irritation and reduce pain. The tannins in tea may also help reduce bleeding and discomfort. Regularly drinking herbal tea may help ease gum swelling symptoms.
faq_safe: Herbal teas like peppermint and chamomile can help with gum swelling, but it's not safe to drink them every day. Some herbal teas can cause allergic reactions or interact with medications. It's best to limit consumption to a few times a week to avoid any potential problems.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like peppermint and ginger can help reduce gum swelling. It may take a few days to a week to see noticeable results. Drinking them regularly can help soothe and calm inflamed gums. As you continue to drink them, you may notice your gums becoming less swollen and more comfortable over time.
faq_time: For gum swelling, it's often best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This allows the active ingredients to work throughout the day, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation. Drinking it in the morning also gives you a chance to see the effects as the day progresses.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for gum swelling. Some teas, like ginseng and licorice root, can increase blood pressure and worsen swelling. Others, like echinacea, can affect blood thinners, which could lead to more bleeding in your gums. It's best to talk to your doctor about any herbal teas you're taking.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to talk to a doctor before drinking herbal teas for gum swelling. Some herbal teas can cause problems during pregnancy. For example, sage tea can slow down milk production and may not be safe. Other teas might affect the baby's development. It's better to be safe and choose other remedies instead.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for gum swelling while breastfeeding can be a safe choice. Many herbs, such as peppermint and chamomile, are commonly used and considered gentle. However, some herbs like sage and dandelion root may cause issues with milk supply. It's a good idea to research the specific herbs you're interested in and understand their potential effects.
title_2: Gum Swelling in Smokers: 9 Herbal Essential Oils for Quick Relief
